backlinks.title: Yang Numbers Have Ended, and When Ended They Rise Again / 陽數已訖，訖則復起
Yang Establishes Through Three, Yin Connects Through Eight / 陽以三立，陰以八通

content:
## English

**Zhou Liu Liu Yao** (周流六爻, "circulating through the six yao") denotes the cyclical movement of the six lines of a hexagram in the alternation of yin and yang. It appears in Chapter 18 of the *Zhouyi Cantong Qi* (《周易參同契》), associated with the operation of the *Xuanji* (璇璣). The *Xuanji* rotates, traversing the six lines in a continuous, revolving motion. This movement is described as "difficult to perceive and hard to observe" (難可察睹), yet it underlies all transformation in the world. The concept is intimately linked to *huishuo hefu* (晦朔合符), the convergence of new and full moons, where the extreme of yang gives rise to yin, and the extreme of yin gives rise to yang. The six yao circulate endlessly in this manner, embodying the principle of "no fixed position" (故無常位). Wei Boyang employed this doctrine to explain the alternation of fire phases in alchemy: the practitioner follows the circulation of the *Xuanji*, advancing and retreating without losing correctness, and through the recurring phases of yin and yang, achieves the work of the Golden Elixir.

## 中文

**周流六爻**，見《[[周易參同契]] · 十八章》。[[璇璣]]運轉，周流於六爻之中，迭運不息，難可察睹。[[晦朔合符]]之際，陽極生陰，陰極生陽，六爻周流莫非此迭運之理。魏伯陽以六爻之周流，明天道之無常位，示丹道之進退循時。修煉者法[[璇璣]]之運，周流六爻，知進退存亡而不失其正，三期迭用，乃成金丹之功。

## 日本語

**周流六爻**（しゅうりゅうりくよう）、《[[周易参同契]]》十八章に見える。[[璇璣]]の运转により、六爻の中を周流し、迭運してやまない。[[晦朔合符]]のとき、陽の極まりて陰を生じ、陰の極まりて陽を生ずる。六爻の周流は莫非この迭運の理による。魏伯陽は六爻の周流をもって天道の変化を示し、丹道における火候の進退を説いた。修煉者は[[璇璣]]の運に従い、六爻を周流し、进退存亡を知りて其の正を失わず、三期迭用して金丹の功を成す。
summary: The cyclical movement through the six lines of a hexagram, as described in the context of the celestial mechanism (璇璣) and the alternation of yin and yang.
title: Zhou Liu Liu Yao / 周流六爻
